title: The Red Soil and Reis Monologue
display_title: The Red Soil and Reis Monologue
wikitext:
[]: The Red Soil and Rei's Monologue             Quote Post by unitM » Thu Aug 10, 2017 7:18 pm Hello, it is now my theory to believe that Rei's monologue (RM), as witnessed in episode 14 of NGE, might have been onto something. It's not hard to notice that red soil, a concept first expressed in her poetry, has been the cover for the final movie and the inconclusive 3rd one for some time now: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: The Red Soil and Rei's Monologue             Quote Post by StrokeMeGoat » Sat Aug 12, 2017 7:56 am I'm not sure my thoughts are together enough to comment on anything yet really, but there's also the fact that in EOE, SEELE says something along the lines of "let the Red Earth purification ceremony begin".

[]: Re: The Red Soil and Rei's Monologue             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Aug 13, 2017 5:24 pm I would say blue skies are very important to the Rebuild series. The red/blue color scheme is used a lot, red being used to represent death or lifelessness (red oceans, red earth), while blue represents something else, maybe life or hope (seen with the blue water at the restoration facility in 2.22). The blue skies in Q are heavily contrasted with the red earth. One of the most beautiful shots of the film is when the awakened Unit-13 ascends to the blue skies, just before shrouding it with red from the Gates of Guf. Then there are the two posters provided by the OP. The first of the posters focuses on the lifeless red earth, while the newer poster focuses more on the blue skies, on hope.

[]: Re: The Red Soil and Rei's Monologue             Quote Post by ElMariachi » Sun Aug 13, 2017 7:58 pm There's another red/blue opposition : Blue is the color of WILLE, as seen by the armband most of its members wears as well as the blue logo on Misato's cap and Sakura's beret, while neo-NERV's logo is still red.

[]: Re: The Red Soil and Rei's Monologue             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Aug 13, 2017 8:02 pm ^Now that's one I've never even noticed, though it seems so obvious in retrospect. Red versus Blue. Good catch.

[]: Re: The Red Soil and Rei's Monologue             Quote Post by Settie » Sun Aug 13, 2017 8:03 pm Team red vs team blue just got a whole new meaning!

[]: Re: The Red Soil and Rei's Monologue             Quote Post by Joseki » Sun Aug 13, 2017 8:46 pm Shinji's room in 3.0 has a red floor and blu ceiling: SPOILER: Show

[]: Re: The Red Soil and Rei's Monologue             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Aug 13, 2017 8:53 pm If we're to get into every little instance of red/blue, there's a lot. I focused on the blue skies because it was mentioned in the OP, and particularly related to Rei's NGE monologue. I know it's fun, but let's try to stay focused on the topic.

[]: Re: The Red Soil and Rei's Monologue             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Aug 13, 2017 9:23 pm Both of the uses brought up are entirely relevant and help bring context to the color symbolism. More than topical enough. Since OP references NGE: EoE uses red to represent death and the spirit world, while blue is used to represent life and the physical world. Could also be seen as fantasy vs. reality. Unclear to me if the red vs. blue moments in the TV series are going for something similar.

[]: Re: The Red Soil and Rei's Monologue             Quote Post by Settie » Sun Aug 13, 2017 9:57 pm One thing I've wondered is, given the importance of turning the lifeless red oceans to blue and full of life in 2.0. That it could be a reference or perhaps taken inspiration from red tide events in reality? Since those are most often associated with mass die offs in the areas that it happens.
